req.title: Manager views policy-document
req.statement: An authenticated manager shall be able to view a policy-document through the UI form. Mandatory fields must be validated and an audit record must be written on success.

doc.reasoning: The requirement mandates that an authenticated manager can view a policy-document via /policy-documents, mandatory fields are validated, and an audit record is written on success (HTTP 200/201 with entity id). Error paths cover 400 (validation), 401 (unauthenticated), and 403 (wrong role). Tests are designed using EP (happy-path manager view), NEG (unauthenticated and forbidden-role access), BVA (policy-document id at boundary values), STC (state transitions: unauthenticated -> authenticated -> view), and DT (decision table combining role x auth x field-validity). Each test is self-contained via fixtures and helper placeholders, deterministic, and idempotent.

tests.name: test_manager_views_policy_document_success
preconditions: At least one policy-document exists in the system
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
Query audit log for the view event
expected: Response body contains entity id matching requested document
Audit record exists for the view action by this manager
trace: R-WEB-056: audit record written on success
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record

def test_manager_views_policy_document_success(<manager_token>, <existing_policy_doc_id>, <api_client>):
    doc_id = <existing_policy_doc_id>
    resp = <api_client>.get(
        f"/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<manager_token>}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id") == doc_id, "Entity id mismatch in response"
    assert body.get("title") is not None, "title field must be present"
    audit = get_audit_record(actor_role="manager", resource_id=doc_id, action="view")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written on successful view"

tests.name: test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_policy_document
preconditions: At least one policy-document exists in the system
actions: Send GET /policy-documents/{existing_id} with no Authorization header
expected: Response body contains an error indicating unauthenticated access
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record

def test_unauthenticated_user_cannot_view_policy_document(<existing_policy_doc_id>, <api_client>):
    doc_id = <existing_policy_doc_id>
    resp = <api_client>.get(f"/policy-documents/{doc_id}")
    assert resp.status_code == 401, f"Expected 401, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Response must indicate auth failure"
    audit = get_audit_record(resource_id=doc_id, action="view")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written for unauthenticated access"

tests.name: test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_policy_document
preconditions: User is authenticated but has role 'employee' (not manager)
At least one policy-document exists in the system
actions: Authenticate as employee via <get_auth_token> fixture with role employee
Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains a forbidden/role error message
No audit record is written for this access attempt
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record

def test_non_manager_role_forbidden_from_viewing_policy_document(
    <employee_token>, <existing_policy_doc_id>, <api_client>
):
    doc_id = <existing_policy_doc_id>
    resp = <api_client>.get(
        f"/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<employee_token>}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 403, f"Expected 403, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Response must indicate forbidden access"
    audit = get_audit_record(resource_id=doc_id, action="view")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record must be written for forbidden access"

tests.name: test_view_policy_document_at_minimum_valid_id_boundary
preconditions: A policy-document with the minimum valid id (1) exists
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: Response body contains entity id equal to 1
Audit record is written for the view of id=1
trace: R-WEB-056: audit record written on success
BVA: minimum boundary of valid policy-document id
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record, ensure_policy_doc_exists

MIN_POLICY_DOC_ID = 1

def test_view_policy_document_at_minimum_valid_id_boundary(<manager_token>, <api_client>):
    ensure_policy_doc_exists(doc_id=MIN_POLICY_DOC_ID)
    resp = <api_client>.get(
        f"/policy-documents/{MIN_POLICY_DOC_ID}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<manager_token>}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code == 200, f"Expected 200, got {resp.status_code}"
    body = resp.json()
    assert body.get("id") == MIN_POLICY_DOC_ID, "Entity id must equal minimum boundary id"
    audit = get_audit_record(actor_role="manager", resource_id=MIN_POLICY_DOC_ID, action="view")
    assert audit is not None, "Audit record must be written for minimum boundary id view"

tests.name: test_view_nonexistent_policy_document_returns_400
preconditions: No policy-document exists with id 0 or a non-existent/invalid id
actions: Capture HTTP response status and body
expected: HTTP status is 400 or 404 indicating validation or not-found error
trace: R-WEB-056: Mandatory fields must be validated
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record

INVALID_DOC_ID = 0

def test_view_nonexistent_policy_document_returns_400(<manager_token>, <api_client>):
    resp = <api_client>.get(
        f"/policy-documents/{INVALID_DOC_ID}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {<manager_token>}"}
    )
    assert resp.status_code in (400, 404), (
        f"Expected 400 or 404 for invalid id, got {resp.status_code}"
    )
    body = resp.json()
    assert "error" in body or "detail" in body, "Error detail must be present in response"
    audit = get_audit_record(resource_id=INVALID_DOC_ID, action="view")
    assert audit is None, "No audit record should be written on validation failure"

tests.name: test_authenticated_manager_state_transition_to_view_policy_document
actions: Attempt GET /policy-documents/{id} with manager token -> expect 200
Verify audit record exists only after authenticated view
expected: Authenticated manager request returns 200 with entity id
Audit record exists only for the authenticated successful view
trace: R-WEB-056: audit record written on success
STC: state transition unauthenticated -> authenticated -> viewed
tests.executable:
import pytest
from <helper_fn> import get_audit_record, get_auth_token

def test_authenticated_manager_state_transition_to_view_policy_document(
    <existing_policy_doc_id>, <api_client>
):
    doc_id = <existing_policy_doc_id>
    unauth_resp = <api_client>.get(f"/policy-documents/{doc_id}")
    assert unauth_resp.status_code == 401, "Unauthenticated must return 401"
    assert get_audit_record(resource_id=doc_id, action="view") is None

    token = get_auth_token(role="manager")
    auth_resp = <api_client>.get(
        f"/policy-documents/{doc_id}",
        headers={"Authorization": f"Bearer {token}"}
    )
    assert auth_resp.status_code == 200, f"Authenticated manager must get 200, got {auth_resp.status_code}"
    assert auth_resp.json().get("id") == doc_id
    assert get_audit_record(actor_role="manager", resource_id=doc_id, action="view") is not None
